name: ABBA App Ranking, Fair Opportunity and Peak Performance System
principle: Every app and capability fraction earns rank from verified accomplishments under comparable conditions. Popularity, brand size and prior usage do not guarantee advantage.
fairOpportunityRules: Every eligible app receives a minimum number of comparable trials before permanent ranking conclusions.
New apps enter an unranked qualification pool and are not penalized for having no history.
Apps compete against equivalent capability, delivery, risk, budget and data classes.
Test briefs, test data, time windows, success conditions and budget ceilings are equal within each match class.
ABBA rotates opportunities so dominant apps do not consume all product work.
Low-volume apps receive exploration trials until confidence is sufficient.
Poor performance triggers diagnosis and optimization trials before restriction or retirement.
Apps may be tested as standalone fractions and as members of different teams to separate individual strength from team compatibility.
Scores are adjusted for task difficulty, data sensitivity, human effort, latency requirements and operating constraints.
No app is promoted solely because it belongs to a winning team; its measured contribution must be proven.

rankingLevels.meaning: top performance in a defined capability and condition class
best currently verified configuration for a defined pathway
limited by risk, reliability or policy
no longer selected except for historical evidence

peakProfile.rule: Peak is contextual, not absolute. An app may be peak in one function, region, budget or risk class and ordinary in another.

rules: Reserve test capacity for new, under-tested and improving apps.
Use proven apps for critical delivery while challengers are tested in sandbox, shadow or low-risk work.
Reduce opportunity only with sufficient evidence, not a single failure.
Reopen competition after major provider updates, price changes, security changes or ecosystem pathway changes.

growthRule: Ranking is not merely a table. It directs training, integration investment, team selection, fallback design, optimization work and future product pathways.
truthRule: All rankings must link to test evidence, accomplishments, conditions, confidence and date. No manually inflated rank is valid.
